title: brunfelsia brasiliensis subsp. macrocalyx
intro: Brunfelsia brasiliensis subsp. macrocalyx, also known as the "Ecuadorian Lantana" or "Palo de Cepillo", is a plant species that belongs to the Brunfelsia genus in the Solanaceae family. It is native to the Andean region of Ecuador and Colombia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Brunfelsia brasiliensis subsp. macrocalyx include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
